because.text: Dispositions: W3 (lifelong keys, no revocation) STALE - SPEC v0.3 s5.1 ships rotation (PoP + quorum of already-bound keys, both-keys refuted at the GOV-001 gate), revocation (supersede of the introducing rotation), DAG-ordered key state with conflicted-key exclusion; the exact surface went through a three-family gate that broke two candidate designs. W4 (policy paradox) DISSOLVES: correction exists (new policy blob, supersede, s7 re-litigation with a new outcome fingerprint) - what cannot happen is silent retroactive mutation, which is the format's purpose, and the corrected record shows both the mistake and the fix, signed. W1 (no consensus) is a settled non-goal (s10) and the useful part is answered by s7 settlement within jurisdictions + s9 explicit divergence across them - the review's own comparison table states this correctly before the weaknesses section forgets it. G3 (trust delegated) - scoped, not delegated: local trust config, genesis roots, advisory genesis.json. ACCEPTED: W2 scale (indexed backend behind the same store interface = M3-era work; O(delta) verification already normative), G4 formal spec (TLA+/Alloy on the key-state DAG derivation under partition = first target, shared front with sigma's Lean/Coq entry), why-graph DOT export as good-first-issue. DECLINED: merged CLI/repos - two one-file CLIs beat one that fits in neither.

subject.note: Qwen web holistic review (8/10): key-revocation claim stale by one release, policy-paradox dissolves into the design goal; scale + TLA+ accepted as roadmap
